findings.message: D6 requires updating the roadmap ADR front matter with `superseded_by`, but the related external_guides ADR explicitly says that same roadmap ADR is grandfathered/post-merge and must not receive semantic front-matter changes. That makes the supersede mechanism contradictory; the plan should use a permitted back-reference/new-ADR encoding instead of requiring a forbidden roadmap front-matter update.

The Makefile migration instructions conflict for `conventions-verify-index`: one bullet says `conventions-*` wrapper tasks should be retained with their implementation swapped to Rust, while the next says the `conventions-verify-index` wrapper should be deleted. This leaves the implementation boundary ambiguous for an existing public task name.

The ADR keeps non-git `cargo make` wrappers (`conventions-*`, `architecture-rules-*`, `workspace-tree*`) and only rewires them to Rust commands, but the already indexed cargo-make teardown ADR says native subcommands that can be called directly should not be wrapped by `cargo make`; non-git pass-through wrappers should move to direct `bin/sotp <native>` usage and be deleted. These plan artifacts now give contradictory implementation direction for the same wrapper layer, so implementers could preserve tasks that the newer workflow ADR requires removing.
